guideline: Interpret left and right from the viewer's image perspective, not from the object's own facing direction.
subject: relative position

guideline: For left-right questions, compare the horizontal positions of the two target objects rather than guessing from scene layout.
subject: relative position

guideline: Match the full object description before judging the relation, especially when several similar objects appear in the image.
subject: target identification

guideline: When one target is tiny, distant, or partly cropped, zoom in before deciding its color or side relation.
subject: visual evidence quality

guideline: Localize the reference object first, then compare the target against it; do not answer from a coarse scene impression.
subject: relative position

guideline: Verify color from the target object's visible pixels, not from category priors or nearby background colors.
subject: color attribute

guideline: For multi-color options, confirm both colors belong to the target object instead of mixing target and background colors.
subject: color attribute

guideline: Count distinct visible people or objects carefully; partial shapes and nearby distractors should not be counted as extra instances.
subject: counting

guideline: If the question asks about a depicted object such as a painting or screen, count only what appears inside that depicted region.
subject: counting

guideline: Only decide curly versus straight hair when the hair texture is visible enough; do not guess from identity or pose.
subject: hair attribute

guideline: When the target attribute is occluded or too blurry, avoid overconfident answers based only on typical object appearance.
subject: attribute verification

guideline: For side comparisons, use the relative centers of the named objects even when one object is much larger than the other.
subject: relative position

guideline: Do not answer a left-right question from road direction, body pose, or object orientation; compare only the named entities in the image plane.
subject: relative position

guideline: Treat answer options as candidates to verify, not hints about the likely answer; choose the option best supported by visible evidence.
subject: multiple choice verification
